description_long: This is a round, heavy-duty manhole cover made of cast iron, featuring a grid pattern in the center for traction and drainage. Around the perimeter, there is a raised border with embossed text reading 'AYUNTAMIENTO DE MADRID ALCANTARILLADO' and 'REGISTRO', indicating its municipal use in Madrid's sewer system. The cover is adorned with a series of five-pointed stars and additional alphanumeric markings, likely referencing standards and manufacturing details. Two rectangular slots are present near the center, likely for lifting or removal with specialized tools. The surface is slightly textured, providing grip and reducing slipperiness. The manhole cover sits flush with the surrounding pavement, which appears to be a solid, flat surface.
description: A round, cast iron manhole cover with a grid pattern, embossed municipal text, and star motifs, designed for Madrid's sewer system.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full grid pattern, all inscriptions, and both lifting slots clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the raised border, star motifs, and the depth of the embossed text.
description_view_2: Rear view with the grid pattern and inscriptions partially visible, showing the symmetry of the design.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the circular shape, border details, and the alignment of the text around the edge.
attribute_pattern_design: Central grid pattern with raised squares, surrounded by a ring of embossed text and five-pointed stars, with two rectangular lifting slots.
